hints: An f-string starts with f before the opening quote: print(f"..."). Without the f, curly braces are treated as literal characters.
To substitute a variable, put its name inside curly braces: print(f"Name: {first_name}"). The value of first_name replaces {first_name} in the output.
You can put two variables in one f-string with a space between them: print(f"{first_name} {last_name}") produces 'Ada Lovelace'.
For the Bio line, you need three substitutions in one f-string: first_name, last_name, and language. Build the sentence and drop each variable into its correct position with {}.
